response: The global food system faces mounting pressure as the population is expected to reach 9.7 billion by 2050, demanding a 60% boost in food production, while climate change, water depletion, and soil degradation (affecting 33% of arable land) threaten yields. Agriculture itself drives roughly 25% of greenhouse gas emissions, and about one-third of food produced is wasted. Proposed solutions include precision and vertical farming, alternative proteins, drought-resistant GM crops, regenerative practices, and policy reforms on subsidies and carbon pricing. The central challenge is scaling these innovations while keeping food affordable and accessible to the poorest populations.
